question: For the coffee-to-water ratio in my French press, did I switch to more water per tablespoon of coffee, or less?
ground_truth_answer: You switched to less water (5 ounces) per tablespoon of coffee.

retrieval_results.context:
User uses a French press coffee ratio of 1 tablespoon of coffee per 5 ounces of water
Plans to brew the new coffee beans using a French press
User rinses French press with hot water to remove loose coffee grounds
Regular cleaning of the French press prevents old coffee oils from building up and affecting coffee flavor
User rinses French press thoroughly with hot water after scrubbing
User dries French press with a towel, paying attention to crevices
User soaks French press in hot, soapy water for about 30 minutes
User scrubs French press with a soft‑bristled brush to remove coffee oils
User can sanitize French press by soaking in a bleach solution (1 tbsp bleach per quart water) for 10‑15 minutes
User can soak French press in equal parts water and baking soda for several hours to remove stubborn stains
